harness:minimal.routing: Use only through explicit init-harness or bootstrap-dev commands; default skill install must not write root harness files.
harness:minimal.recommendation: Initialize the standard target harness with stable tracked rules, ignored worktree-local task state, loop control-plane policy/evals, LLM Wiki context pack, validation, and managed ownership only through an explicit bootstrap command.

harness:website-cloner.routing: Use only as an explicit template for authorized website clone smoke tests and report artifacts; default skill install must not write website-cloner harness files.
harness:website-cloner.recommendation: Keep website cloning behind explicit user intent, safety review, browser evidence, and local validation because it uses external sites, file writes, and potentially protected third-party design assets.

skill:workflow-router.routing: Load when a non-trivial request needs intent recognition before work starts; classify it into exactly one workflow owner or ask a clarification.
skill:workflow-router.recommendation: Add a thin executable intent router that selects exactly one workflow owner, runs side-effect-free preflight checks, and audits helper activation boundaries before work starts.

skill:answer-workflow.routing: Use for read-only questions, explanations, evidence lookup, feasibility checks, and comparisons.
skill:answer-workflow.recommendation: Answer read-only questions from evidence without mutating files.

skill:sdd-workflow.routing: Use for feature, bug fix, refactor, product/spec change, or implementation requests that must align spec and acceptance before coding.
skill:sdd-workflow.recommendation: Run the full SDD lifecycle with source gathering, spec, acceptance, executable plan, cleanup, implementation, tests, and handoff.

skill:diagnosis-workflow.routing: Use when a task starts from a failing command, runtime bug, flaky behavior, performance regression, or agent/tool failure.
skill:diagnosis-workflow.recommendation: Reproduce or bound a failure before applying the smallest confirmed fix.

skill:review-workflow.routing: Use for code, plan, release, UI, or security review when findings should come before implementation.
skill:review-workflow.recommendation: Review with findings first and avoid implementing fixes unless explicitly redirected.

skill:delivery-workflow.routing: Use when accepted work needs validation closeout, PR status triage, cleanup, handoff, release notes, or local environment reset.
skill:delivery-workflow.recommendation: Close accepted work and PR updates with validation, PR mergeability and CI/check-run status, in-scope blocker fixes, cleanup, residual-risk notes, and handoff.

skill:hub-maintenance-workflow.routing: Use for maintaining Harness Hub sources, routing, capabilities, npm lifecycle, and managed update/remove behavior.
skill:hub-maintenance-workflow.recommendation: Keep Harness Hub source maintenance local to this repository; do not migrate this workflow into target repositories.

skill:product-capability.routing: Use under SDD when product intent needs constraints, invariants, interfaces, unresolved decisions, and acceptance boundaries before implementation.
skill:product-capability.recommendation: Create an implementation-ready capability contract before coding product-facing changes.

skill:grill-me.routing: Use when the user asks to be grilled, challenged, pressure-tested, or wants assumptions surfaced before implementation.
skill:grill-me.recommendation: Use when the user asks to be grilled, challenged, pressure-tested, or wants assumptions surfaced before implementation.

skill:grill-with-docs.routing: Use when the user explicitly asks for grill-with-docs, asks to be grilled with docs, or wants plan pressure testing that can capture glossary, context wiki, domain-model, ADR, or code/docs contradiction updates; do not load when the skill name is only background context for implementation, maintenance, or review; wiki writes require human confirmation.
skill:grill-with-docs.recommendation: Use as a Harness Hub-compatible grill-with-docs entrypoint that preserves one-question-at-a-time pressure testing while mapping durable knowledge to confirmed LLM Wiki, contradiction, and task-state updates.

skill:diagnose.routing: Use for product, code, test, and performance failures; use agent-introspection-debugging for agent/tool failures, webapp-testing for one-off browser inspection, and verification-loop after the fix.
skill:diagnose.recommendation: Use for product, code, test, and performance failures; use agent-introspection-debugging for agent/tool failures, webapp-testing for one-off browser inspection, and verification-loop after the fix.

skill:prototype.routing: Use only for clearly marked throwaway prototypes that answer one design question; use frontend-design for production UI, web-artifacts-builder for standalone artifacts, and tdd-workflow for production implementation.
skill:prototype.recommendation: Use only for clearly marked throwaway prototypes that answer one design question; use frontend-design for production UI, web-artifacts-builder for standalone artifacts, and tdd-workflow for production implementation.

skill:effective-interact.routing: Load when agent pauses to report relatively complex information needing Chinese-first clear complex communication, communication-density discipline, alignment, multi-option choice, status/incident, long-task fact ledgers, implementation plans, reviews, maps, explainers, evidence, risks, validation, handoff, proactive HTML reports, visibly polished structured text, or trigger-hardening complaints, including broad repo capability/structure/function/implementation maps and html-effectiveness families such as Exploration & Planning, Code Review & Understanding, Design, Reports, and Custom Editors; organize from visual language first and read DESIGN.md for component-first HTML artifacts, then choose plain text/Markdown/visual Markdown/HTML by decision cost, defaulting material repo/skill changes to HTML handoff; use Ponytail for coding minimalism; skip trivial chat/bundled apps.
skill:effective-interact.recommendation: Structure Chinese-first complex pause reports when material alignment, handoffs, option comparisons, long-task fact ledgers, reviews, PR writeups, implementation plans, status or incident dashboards, evidence, risks, validation, or continuation notes need first-principles organization; apply communication-density checks before expanding into prose or artifacts, start from useful visual language and the component-first DESIGN.md contract, keep any non-plain-brief output visibly structured instead of linear prose, avoid Markdown-in-HTML, use Ponytail for coding minimization, and default material repo/skill change handoffs to validated self-contained HTML unless explicitly waived.

skill:handoff.routing: Use when the user asks to hand off current work, compact this conversation for another agent/session, or create restart notes; use effective-interact for visual HTML repo reports.
skill:handoff.recommendation: Create concise restart notes in the OS temp directory so another agent or later session can continue from verified facts.

skill:compound-code-review.routing: Use for deep pre-PR or CE-style code review. Use security-review for focused security checklists, web-design-guidelines for UI/UX audits, and verification-loop for final command gates.
skill:compound-code-review.recommendation: Use for deep pre-PR or CE-style code review. Use security-review for focused security checklists, web-design-guidelines for UI/UX audits, and verification-loop for final command gates.

skill:quick-learn.routing: Load when the user explicitly wants a source-backed learning project, custom study plan, syllabus, book or technical topic coaching, teach-back, quizzes, or staged review; do not route ordinary implementation, one-off factual lookup, or article writing.
skill:quick-learn.recommendation: Use for explicit source-backed learning projects with confirmed custom syllabus, stage-level review, active assessment, and durable .quick-learn logs.

skill:coding-standards.routing: Use when a workflow-router-selected owner workflow needs cross-project code quality conventions after scope is selected; do not use as a top-level owner.

skill:karpathy-guidelines.routing: Use when a workflow-router-selected owner workflow needs a coding behavior baseline for writing, reviewing, or refactoring code; do not use as a top-level owner, requirements workflow, TDD workflow, or code review workflow.
skill:karpathy-guidelines.recommendation: Use as a helper baseline for assumption surfacing, simple implementation, surgical diffs, and verification criteria inside the selected owner workflow.

skill:ponytail.routing: Load when coding work should be deliberately minimal: YAGNI, reuse existing code, prefer stdlib/native/already-installed dependencies, shortest correct diff, no unrequested abstractions, or over-engineering review; use effective-interact for non-coding reports and communication artifacts, tdd-workflow for lifecycle, and security-review for focused security.
skill:ponytail.recommendation: Use as the standard coding-minimalism helper so implementation and review work default to YAGNI, reuse, stdlib/native/already-installed dependencies, smallest correct diffs, and short code-first explanations without taking over reporting, lifecycle, security, or requested walkthroughs.

skill:tdd-workflow.routing: Use for production features, confirmed bug fixes, and refactors that should proceed one behavior at a time through red-green-refactor; use diagnose for unknown root causes and prototype for throwaway exploration.
skill:tdd-workflow.recommendation: Drive production changes one behavior at a time through public behavior tests, minimal implementation, and refactor-after-green.

skill:verification-loop.routing: Use for final build, typecheck, lint, test, diff, smoke, and artifact validation gates after work is done; use diagnosis/review owners before root-cause analysis.

skill:security-review.routing: Use for focused review of security-sensitive code, auth, secrets, injection, unsafe IO, payments, or privacy risk; do not use for general review.
skill:security-review.recommendation: Security-sensitive code, auth, secrets, injection, unsafe IO, or payments.

skill:documentation-lookup.routing: Use when current library, framework, SDK, API, CLI, or cloud-service documentation is needed; use answer-workflow for ordinary repo-local explanation.
skill:documentation-lookup.recommendation: Library, framework, SDK, API, CLI, or cloud-service docs.

skill:package-release-sniffer.routing: Load when tracking newly published package or model-package releases across package registries and release feeds for AI or developer-tool monitoring; use documentation-lookup for docs on a chosen package and implementation workflows for building registry clients.
skill:package-release-sniffer.recommendation: Add source-first package release sniffing for AI and developer-tool monitoring without installing registry clients, hooks, credentials, or scheduled monitors.

skill:harness-quality-check.routing: Load when explicitly asked to audit Harness Hub or target repo harness quality/readiness and produce advisory HTML findings; use verification-loop for final gates, coding-standards for code quality conventions, skill-creator for skill authoring, and hub-maintenance-workflow for actual source changes.
skill:harness-quality-check.recommendation: Compose existing Harness Hub self-check, readiness, harness validation, and skill-quality evidence into local advisory HTML reports without remote writes, schedules, hooks, or enforcement changes.

skill:agent-introspection-debugging.routing: Use when a workflow-router-selected owner workflow needs agent run, tool loop, context drift, or recoverable harness/tool failure debugging after product bugs are ruled out.
skill:agent-introspection-debugging.recommendation: Repeated agent failures, loops, drift, or recoverable tool failures.

skill:frontend-design.routing: Use for polished product UI or web pages, not routine interaction artifacts.
skill:frontend-design.recommendation: Use for polished product UI or web pages, not routine interaction artifacts.

skill:design-taste-frontend.routing: Use for anti-template visual direction, design-read calibration, and pre-flight critique for landing pages, portfolios, marketing pages, and redesigns; use frontend-design for production UI implementation, frontend-patterns for logic, web-artifacts-builder for standalone artifacts, effective-interact for reports, and do not use for dashboards, data tables, or multi-step product UI.
skill:design-taste-frontend.recommendation: Use as a narrow anti-slop visual taste layer for landing pages, portfolios, marketing pages, and redesigns.

skill:clone-website.routing: Load when the user explicitly asks to clone, reverse-engineer, or rebuild an authorized website; do not use for generic redesign, visual inspiration, scraping, phishing, impersonation, or third-party brand copying.
skill:clone-website.recommendation: Use as an explicit website-clone workflow only after permission, browser automation, replacement-content, and validation gates are clear.

skill:web-artifacts-builder.routing: Use for complex standalone React/Tailwind/shadcn browser artifacts; use frontend-design for production UI and effective-interact for communication reports.

skill:frontend-slides.routing: Use for HTML presentations, slide decks, talk or pitch slides, and PPT/PPTX-to-web conversion; use effective-interact only for deck summaries or handoff.
skill:frontend-slides.recommendation: Use for slide decks and PPT/PPTX-to-web work.

skill:frontend-patterns.routing: Use for React or Next.js frontend logic, state, forms, routing, accessibility, or responsive behavior; use frontend-design for visual production UI.
skill:frontend-patterns.recommendation: Use for React, state, forms, routing, and accessibility patterns.

skill:webapp-testing.routing: Use for one-off local web app inspection, screenshots, console logs, UI reproduction, or agent-run Web browser acceptance; use e2e-testing for durable Playwright suites.
skill:webapp-testing.recommendation: One-off local browser inspection and agent-run Web browser acceptance.

skill:web-design-guidelines.routing: Use for UI, UX, accessibility, or web interface guideline compliance review; use frontend-design when creating or changing production UI.

skill:e2e-testing.routing: Use for durable Playwright E2E suites, Page Object Models, fixtures, CI browser tests, or flaky-test strategy; use webapp-testing for one-off inspection.
skill:e2e-testing.recommendation: Durable E2E suites and CI browser tests.

skill:openspec-explore.routing: Use only for explicit OpenSpec exploration, discovery, or requirement clarification; ordinary SDD/product planning should use product-capability or sdd-workflow.
skill:openspec-explore.recommendation: Explicit OpenSpec exploration or formal change lifecycle only.

skill:openspec-propose.routing: Use only for explicit new OpenSpec proposals with design, spec deltas, and tasks; ordinary capability plans should use product-capability or sdd-workflow.

skill:openspec-apply-change.routing: Use only for implementing or continuing an existing OpenSpec change; use tdd-workflow or verification-loop for non-OpenSpec implementation and checks.

skill:openspec-archive-change.routing: Use only for finalizing and archiving completed OpenSpec changes after implementation and spec sync decisions are resolved.

skill:claude-api.routing: Use for Claude API or Anthropic SDK implementation, debugging, tuning, and migration; verify live provider docs before code changes.
skill:claude-api.recommendation: Add explicit Anthropic API support without loading volatile API tables into default workflows.

skill:mcp-builder.routing: Use for designing, building, reviewing, or testing MCP servers and tool contracts for external services.
skill:mcp-builder.recommendation: Add a bounded MCP server builder atom with reference guides and evaluation scripts.

skill:skill-creator.routing: Use for creating, adapting, updating, or evaluating standard agent skills after the owner workflow has selected the maintenance/change lane.
skill:skill-creator.recommendation: Add a skill authoring atom for local skills and routing evaluation without host-specific eval runners.

skill:source-post.routing: Load when turning external articles, blogs, release notes, interviews, or reports into source-backed public posts with Chinese fidelity, media references, effective-interact summary, and project-iteration review; do not use for ordinary summaries, full copyrighted reposts, or production site design.
skill:source-post.recommendation: Use as a bounded source-to-public-blog workflow that preserves source evidence, media references, and iteration decisions while forcing reader-first article flow; do not turn effective-interact reports, source ledgers, or copyrighted source text into the main post.

skill:agent-interaction-audit.routing: Load when the user asks for a private agent interaction audit, repository interaction audit, cross-session Codex or Claude Code trace analysis, agent task profile, tool-call decision audit, collaboration bottleneck analysis, prompt/rule context audit, prompt/rule drift audit, automation log audit or review, closeout retrospective, agent-interaction-retro evidence, SOP/script lesson mining, knowledge-cache/eval/workflow-change candidates, executable improvement queue, or concrete improvement recommendations from local agent traces; legacy insight mentions route here only when paired with sessions, traces, audits, recommendations, improvements, or bottlenecks. Use effective-interact as the visual presentation layer for dense reports, source-post for public external-source posts, agent-introspection-debugging for single-run failures, and harness-quality-check for advisory harness readiness audits.
skill:agent-interaction-audit.recommendation: Audit recent project-related Codex and Claude Code collaboration traces, prompt/rule layers, automation logs, stale guidance, closeout retrospectives, SOP lessons, repeated agent mistakes, eval/workflow-change candidates, and cacheable knowledge candidates into ignored private reports plus private improvement queue JSON with confirmed/candidate evidence separation, optional effective-interact visual report inputs for dense audits, and no default project, memory, schedule, remote, or tracked-doc mutation.

skill:doc-coauthoring.routing: Use for collaboratively drafting, restructuring, and reader-testing PRDs, RFCs, proposals, specs, and decision records.
skill:doc-coauthoring.recommendation: Fill the durable document coauthoring gap with a local original atom; upstream Anthropic text was not copied due missing per-skill license.

skill:internal-comms.routing: Use for internal status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, and project updates.
skill:internal-comms.recommendation: Add an internal communications atom with reusable reference formats.

skill:stop-slop.routing: Load when the user explicitly asks to draft, edit, or review English prose for AI-writing tells; do not use for code explanations, Chinese output, technical specs, or routine status reports.
skill:stop-slop.recommendation: Install a narrow English prose cleanup skill for AI-writing tells; treat its bans on adverbs, passive voice, Wh- openers, and em dashes as strong style-editing rules, not default documentation policy.

skill:theme-factory.routing: Use when an existing slide, document, report, HTML artifact, or landing page needs a coherent visual theme.
skill:theme-factory.recommendation: Add a bounded visual theming atom that complements, rather than replaces, production UI design.

skill:slack-gif-creator.routing: Use for creating, optimizing, or validating animated GIFs for Slack emoji or messages.
skill:slack-gif-creator.recommendation: Add a narrow creative atom for Slack-ready GIF generation and validation.
